ent_ff1fa4cd.evidence_quote: A set volume of air-dried soil was placed in a 30-mesh concave sieve and immersed by hand in deionized water 20 times over 15 seconds to disintegrate water-sensitive particles.

ent_7a3b7384.name: Scanning Grid
ent_7a3b7384.description: A tool used to measure the surface area of stable soil aggregates after immersion in water.
ent_7a3b7384.evidence_quote: Remaining stable aggregates were then measured on a scanning grid, with accuracy to the nearest 0.25 cm in diameter.

ent_976e4c73.name: Rubber Spatula Aggregate Breakdown
ent_976e4c73.description: A manual method to break down non-stable soil aggregates after immersion in water.
ent_976e4c73.evidence_quote: The soil was then dipped once more, and the aggregates were manually broken down with a small rubber spatula.

ent_0d58086c.name: Surface-Area Grid
ent_0d58086c.description: A tool used to measure the surface area of non-aggregate sand particles after manual breakdown of soil aggregates.
ent_0d58086c.evidence_quote: Non-aggregate sand particles were subsequently measured on the surface-area grid.

ent_86e6f82e.name: Water-Stable Aggregate Calculation
ent_86e6f82e.description: A calculation method to determine the stability of soil aggregates by comparing surface areas before and after immersion in water.
ent_86e6f82e.evidence_quote: Water-stable aggregate calculation involved the difference between the initial surface area before dipping and the /uniFB01 nal area after the second dip, divided by the initial fresh surface area before dipping.

ent_86dcafbc.name: Soil Core Sampling
ent_86dcafbc.description: A method to collect undisturbed soil samples for analysis of moisture characteristics and other properties.
ent_86dcafbc.evidence_quote: Soil core samples were collected at the end of a 4-year crop rotation cycle on 23 August 2022, using a cylindrical ring with a volume of 153.9 cm³.

ent_916ff266.name: Tempe Cells
ent_916ff266.description: Equipment used to apply varying air pressures to soil cores to measure soil moisture at different suction levels.
ent_916ff266.evidence_quote: The soil cores were subjected to varying air pressures (0 to ~-914 cm [H20]) in tempe cells, and the soil moisture at different pressures was recorded.

ent_ca24e44f.name: Van Genuchten Model
ent_ca24e44f.description: A mathematical model used to fit soil moisture characteristic curves by relating soil water content to soil suction.
ent_ca24e44f.evidence_quote: The soil moisture characteristics curve was then /uniFB01 tted using the Van Genuchten model as outlined in Equation 3 where, q is soil water content (cm 3 cm -3 ), q s is saturated water content (cm 3 cm -3 ), q r is soil residual water content (cm 3 cm -3 ), n is a dimensionless pore size distribution measure, and m is a shape parameters, a is inverse of air entry suction (cm -1 ) ( a > 0), and h is the soil suction (cm of H20) (Van Genuchten, 1980).

ent_ab9ee7f3.name: Rosetta Lite v 1.1
ent_ab9ee7f3.description: A neural network-based software used to predict soil water retention characteristics using soil texture and bulk density data.
ent_ab9ee7f3.evidence_quote: The initial estimates for these retention parameters were derived using the Rosetta Lite v 1.1 software (Schaap et al., 2001). It employs a neural network-based prediction method that utilizes the soil texture percentages (sand, silt, clay) and bulk density values to estimate the water retention characteristics of soils.

ent_f094e7fc.name: Brooks and Corey Model
ent_f094e7fc.description: A mathematical model used to fit soil water retention curves by relating soil water content to air entry pressure and shape parameters.
ent_f094e7fc.evidence_quote: Moreover, left part of Equations 3 and 4 denote effective water saturation. Curve /uniFB01 tting was done using RETC, Version 6.02 (Van Genuchten et al., 1991). Oven dry weight was calculated by placing the soil in oven at 105°C for 24 hrs. Particle density was assumed to be 2.65 g cm -3 .
